description:
Search Unity assets in the pre-built index. Use this to find prefabs, scenes, materials, textures, scripts, animations, etc.

Query syntax: space-separated predicates, AND semantics between predicates. Wrap predicate values in double quotes when they contain spaces, e.g. 'n:"Point Light"'. Use \" and \| for literal quotes and pipes.
IMPORTANT: use | (pipe) inside ANY predicate value for OR semantics (pipes split only outside quoted text; works for both type and name fields). Always combine related types/names into a single call, e.g. 't:prefab|script n:player|hero' searches prefabs AND scripts whose name contains 'player' or 'hero' — in ONE call.

All name predicates (n=, n^, n$, n:) are CASE-INSENSITIVE.

Supported predicates:
  t:<type|type2>   — filter by asset type
  n=<name|name2>   — exact filename match
  n^<pfx|pfx2>     — filename starts with
  n$<sfx|sfx2>     — filename ends with
  n:<sub|sub2>     — filename contains substring
  under:<pathPfx>  — asset path starts with prefix (e.g. 'Assets/UI')
  guid:<guid>      — exact GUID match (32-hex)

Asset types: scene, prefab, material, animation, animatorController, genericAsset, script, texture, audio, shader, model, otherYaml, metaOnly

Examples:
  't:prefab under:Assets/UI'               — all prefabs under Assets/UI
  't:prefab n:PointLight|"Point Light"'    — compact or spaced names
  't:prefab|scene'                          — all prefabs and scenes

Output: first line is `total:<N> showing:<start>-<end>`, then one tab-separated line per result. Default fields: p (path only); request more with the 'fields' param.

q.description: Query string with space-separated predicates. Quote values that contain spaces. Example: 't:prefab under:Assets/UI n:"Point Light"'

range.description: Row range as '<start>-<end>' (1-indexed, max 200 rows). Examples: '1-20' (first 20), '21-40' (next 20), '0' (count only). Default: '1-20'.

fields.description: Fields per result line (tab-separated). Fewer fields = fewer tokens. Available: tp, n, p, guid. `tp` shows `ClassName(genericAsset)` for ScriptableObject assets when indexed script metadata is available.
